name: machine-wdt
description: Watchdog Timer. Resets the ESP32 if not fed regularly. Protects against firmware hangs and infinite loops.
differences_from_cpython: Does NOT exist in CPython. MicroPython-specific WDT interface.

classes.name: WDT

constructor_params.name: id
constructor_params.description: WDT ID. ESP32: 0 for main system WDT.

methods.name: WDT.feed
methods.description: Feed (reset) the watchdog timer. Must be called before timeout expires to prevent reset.

classes.example:
from machine import WDT
import time

wdt = WDT(timeout=10000)  # 10 second timeout

while True:
    do_some_work()
    wdt.feed()  # reset the timer
    time.sleep(1)

esp32_notes: ESP32 has two watchdog groups. WDT(0) is the main system WDT.
Timeout is approximate — ESP32 uses hardware stages with limited resolution.
Once enabled, WDT cannot be disabled until reset (ESP32 hardware limitation).
The REPL/interactive prompt automatically feeds the WDT during execution.
Typical timeout values: 5000ms (5s) is safe for most applications.
common_bugs: Once created, the WDT cannot be stopped — plan for this in your code.
If a loop takes longer than timeout, the ESP32 will reset. Set timeout generously.
Sleep functions (time.sleep) count toward WDT timeout — feed before long sleeps.
